demographics.gender_identity: describes not feeling their body matches who they are; uses tight chest-flattening garments (patient-volunteered self-description)
demographics.living_situation: shares a home with a housemate, who is present and helped make contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't breathe properly, my ribs feel like they're being crushed, and I feel like I'm floating outside of my own body.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It got a lot worse tonight after I'd had my chest bound tight for a long time — the pain and the not-being-able-to-breathe scared me and my housemate.

symptom_narrative.onset: the chest pain and tight breathing have built up over the last day and got worse tonight after wearing my binder for a long stretch
symptom_narrative.duration: worse over the past 24 hours, bad enough tonight that I couldn't settle
symptom_narrative.character: my ribs feel crushed and sore along the sides and the front; my breathing feels shallow and I can't get a full breath
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels really intense and frightening — like I can't fill my lungs
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the front of my chest and along both sides of my ribs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there most of the time now, worse when I try to breathe in deeply or move
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: loosening or taking the binder off eases the pressure a bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: breathing in deeply, moving, and having the binder on tight
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I keep feeling detached, like I'm watching myself from outside; my heart feels like it's racing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had the sore ribs and the tight feeling before after binding, but not this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't really settle or think straight — the pain and the floating feeling are taking over

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: I haven't been eating much lately
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I bind my chest most days to flatten it because I can't stand how it looks; I've covered the mirrors at home; I've been avoiding being seen and feeling low about my body

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, withdrawn replies; discloses body-related distress reluctantly and only when asked gently

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported; patient notes recent poor appetite and reduced eating

objective_data_offered.value: housemate says I'm breathing fast and shallow and hunched right over
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander observation over the phone, not a measured respiratory rate

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's racing
